title: Indulgent Smoky Slovakian Cox-Apple Grill
description: A fresh, springy, lazy-sunday Slovakian dish featuring smoky apple slices and crisp tofu, all cooked on a tava-griddle for a sugar-free indulgent bite.

ingredients.name: cox apples
ingredients.amount: 2 medium, peeled, cored and sliced 1/4" thick

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.amount: 200 g, pressed and cut into 1/2" cubes

instructions: Preheat a tava-griddle over medium-high heat until hot.
Whisk smoked paprika, smoked sea salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and erythritol in a bowl.
Toss tofu cubes in the spice mix until evenly coated.
Drizzle apple slices with 1 teaspoon olive oil and sprinkle a pinch of smoked paprika.
Add 1 tablespoon olive oil to the heated griddle and swirl to coat.
Place tofu cubes in a single layer and cook for 4-5 minutes, flipping once until golden brown.
Add apple slices to the same griddle, cooking 2-3 minutes per side until caramelized.
Drizzle tamari sauce over tofu and apples, allowing it to caramelize for 30 seconds.
Transfer all items to a serving platter.
Garnish with fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice for brightness.
Serve immediately while hot with a side of steamed greens or quinoa if desired.
Enjoy a smoky, sugar-free Slovakian-inspired brunch dish.
